channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Police say FBI agent sexually assaulted 2 women after promise of free tattoos, modeling
item.description: GAITHERSBURG, Md. (AP) — An FBI agent charged in Maryland with sexually assaulting two women contacted his alleged victims through social media with a promise to give them free tattoos and modeling work, police said Tuesday as they encouraged other potential victims to come forward.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's threat to impose tariffs could raise prices for consumers, colliding with promise for relief
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — If Donald Trump makes good on his threat to slap 25% tariffs on everything imported from Mexico and Canada, the price increases that could follow will collide with his campaign promise to give American families a break from inflation.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court agrees to end Trump’s classified documents case
item.description: The 11th Circuit Court of Appeals on Tuesday granted a request from special counsel Jack Smith to drop President-elect Trump’s Florida documents case, ending the battle to charge him over retaining records with classified markings after leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire to end nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel approved a United States-brokered ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah on Tuesday that would end nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: San Francisco watchdog Ricci Wynne arrested, suspected of pimping
item.description: Wynne has legions of social media followers. Over the past three years, videos posted on his social media pages, RawRicci415, garnered hundreds of thousands of views.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KREX 5

item.title: Court rejects request to sideline San Jose State volleyball player on grounds she’s transgender
item.description: A federal appeals court upheld a ruling Tuesday that allows a San Jose State women’s volleyball team member to play in this week’s Mountain West Conference tournament after a legal complaint said she should be ineligible on grounds that she is transgender and thus stronger, posing a safety risk to t
item.dc:creator: AMY BETH HANSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Surveillance tech advances by Biden could aid in Trump's promised crackdown on immigration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration — among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, GARANCE BURKE and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How Trump's bet on voters electing him managed to silence some of his legal woes
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith move to abandon two federal cases accusing Trump of endangering American democracy and national security does away with the most serious legal threats Trump was facing as he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DEA seizes 670K fentanyl pills headed to Denver
item.description: The Drug Enforcement Agency said Monday that a tip led to the seizure of around 670,000 fake pills headed to Denver from Albuquerque.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, NewsNation

item.title: Tim Walz accepts turkey presentation as he eases back into his duties as Minnesota's governor
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — As feathers flew, Tim Walz accepted the symbolic presentation of a turkey Tuesday as he eases back into his duties as Minnesota's governor following the Harris-Walz ticket's defeat in the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police officer rescues sleeping man after rolling off bridge
item.description: Authorities with the Oklahoma Police Department say, a rescue incident led to one man being saved in just the nick of time.
item.dc:creator: Terré Gables/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rudy Giuliani draws rebuke for a courtroom outburst accusing judge in assets case of being unfair
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In an angry outburst in a New York courtroom, Rudy Giuliani accused a judge Tuesday of making wrong assumptions about him as he tries to comply with an order requiring him to turn over most of his assets to two election poll workers who won a libel case against him. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thanksgiving Travel Latest: TSA braces for ‘busiest ever’ holiday
item.description: The Thanksgiving travel rush is expected to be bigger than ever this year. AAA predicts that nearly 80 million people in the U.S. will venture at least 50 miles (80 kilometers) from home between Tuesday and next Monday. Another round of wintry weather could complicate travel, though.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden proposes Medicare and Medicaid cover costly weight-loss drugs for millions of obese Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Millions of Americans with obesity would be eligible to have popular weight-loss drugs like Wegovy or Zepbound covered by Medicare or Medicaid under a new rule the Biden administration proposed Tuesday morning. The costly proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden's public lands director named to lead environmental group
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — A senior official in President Joe Biden's administration who oversaw its contentious efforts to address climate change by curbing oil drilling on federal lands while expanding renewable power was named Tuesday as the next president of a prominent environmental group. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dog treats distributed to 19 states recalled for potential salmonella
item.description: A chicken-based dog treat sold online and in stores across 19 states has been recalled over the potential risk of salmonella.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, KRON4

item.title: Israel, Lebanon's Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire to end nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israel approved a United States-brokered ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah on Tuesday, setting the stage for an end to nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the ongoing war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: HIV-positive organ transplants allowed under new rule
item.description: People with HIV will soon be permitted to receive transplanted kidneys and livers from donors who are also infected with the virus under a new rule approved by the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Here's where Trump's criminal cases stand
item.description: President-elect Trump’s legal strategy of delay is paying off, with his election victory changing the trajectory of his four criminal prosecutions.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Texas offers Trump administration more land to stage mass deportations
item.description: “We have 13 million acres around the state, and if there’s something that meets the federal government’s needs, we want them to be able to utilize that,” Land Commissioner Dawn Buckingham said.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Berenice Garcia, KPRC 2

item.title: Veteran living in driveway after hurricanes Helene, Milton destroyed home
item.description: A U.S. Army veteran is grappling with his new reality after Helene and Milton destroyed his New Port Richey home.
item.dc:creator: Linnie Supall, NewsNation

item.title: American, Frontier planes clip wings at Logan International Airport in Boston
item.description: Officials confirmed that two planes operated by American Airlines and Frontier Airlines clipped wings on the tarmac at Logan International Airport in Boston on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Kentucky sheriff pleads not guilty in the fatal courthouse shooting of a judge
item.description: A former Kentucky sheriff charged in a judge's shooting death pleaded not guilty Monday in the same courthouse where they worked together and where the attack occurred, and one of his attorneys later predicted they will present a compelling case in his defense.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A voting machine firm suing Fox News now wants to probe Murdoch family trust fight
item.description: Lawyers for a voting machine company that’s suing Fox News want to question founder Rupert Murdoch about his contentious efforts to change his family trust, the attorneys told a court Monday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walmart ordered to pay former truck driver $35M after jury finds fraud claims false
item.description: A jury in California found Walmart defamed a truck driver with false claims of workers' compensation fraud and will have to pay the former worker more than $34 million in damages.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Former dental assistant charged with sexually assaulting minors
item.description: A man who worked several months as an assistant at an oral surgery center allegedly recorded himself sexually assaulting 16 young males.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Bird flu detected in batch of California raw milk, company issues recall, health officials say
item.description: The bird flu virus was detected in a batch of raw milk that originated at a California dairy farm, public health officials said over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Volunteers join a search for a woman from Hawaii missing in LA for weeks as her father is found dead
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Volunteers have joined a search in Los Angeles for Hannah Kobayashi, a 30-year-old woman from Hawaii who went missing more than two weeks ago after not making a connecting flight to New York.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bird flu detected in raw milk from California, state officials warn
item.description: The avian influenza virus has been detected in a retail sample of raw milk from a California-based raw dairy farm, California health officials confirmed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Varela, WGN 9

item.title: Don't be 'that guy' at the TSA airport security checkpoint
item.description: While celebrating the holiday with family can bring its own stresses, nothing quite compares to the frenzy of the airport security checkpoint.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Photo said to show Amelia Earhart's plane is something else entirely, exploration company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Special counsel moves to dismiss election interference case against President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith asked a federal judge on Monday to dismiss the case accusing President-elect Donald Trump of plotting to overturn the 2020 election, citing longstanding Justice Department policy shielding presidents from prosecution while in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: First right whales of season gorge on critical food off Massachusetts, giving hope for a strong year
item.description: Scientists who study a critically endangered species of whale that lives off New England said encouraging early signs suggest the animals could have a strong season for feeding and breeding.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dentist accused of fatally poisoning wife hit with new charges as lawyer quits
item.description: James Toliver Craig is accused of killing his wife of 23 years, Angela Craig, by putting poison in her protein shakes – he now faces two additional charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1 dead after DHL cargo plane crashes in Lithuania; officials 'cannot rule out' terrorism
item.description: Lithuanian officials said they were investigating the cause of the crash, and "cannot rule out" terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Siarhei Satsiuk, WGN 9

item.title: Chiefs' Patrick Mahomes fined over $14K for 'violent gesture' at Bills game
item.description: The fine comes after the star quarterback celebrated a touchdown pass with a hand gesture that appeared to imitate him holding a gun.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, WFLA, NBC
